verses.text: When any will offer a meat offering — fine flour, oil, frankincense — bring it to Aaron's sons. The priest takes a handful with oil and all the frankincense, burns it on the altar as a memorial — sweet savor to the LORD. The remnant is Aaron's and his sons' — most holy. Guidance: Not all offerings are bloody. Flour, oil, frankincense — bread the worshipper grew with his own hands. God receives a fistful as remembrance; the priests eat the rest.

When any will offer a meat offering — fine flour, oil, frankincense — bring it to Aaron's sons. The priest takes a handful with oil and all the frankincense, burns it on the altar as a memorial — sweet savor to the LORD. The remnant is Aaron's and his sons' — most holy. Guidance: Not all offerings are bloody. Flour, oil, frankincense — bread the worshipper grew with his own hands. God receives a fistful as remembrance; the priests eat the rest.

When any will offer a meat offering — fine flour, oil, frankincense — bring it to Aaron's sons. The priest takes a handful with oil and all the frankincense, burns it on the altar as a memorial — sweet savor to the LORD. The remnant is Aaron's and his sons' — most holy. Guidance: Not all offerings are bloody. Flour, oil, frankincense — bread the worshipper grew with his own hands. God receives a fistful as remembrance; the priests eat the rest.

If your oblation be baked in the oven — unleavened cakes mingled with oil, or wafers anointed with oil. If of the pan — unleavened with oil. If of the frying pan — fine flour with oil. Bring to the LORD; priest takes the memorial, burns it — sweet savor. The remnant is Aaron's — most holy. Guidance: Three ways to prepare it — oven, pan, frying pan. God receives the bread however you cook it; what matters is the oil and the heart that brings it.

God receives the bread however you cook it; what matters is the oil and the heart that brings it.

No meat offering with leaven; neither leaven nor honey burned on the altar. As oblation of firstfruits offer them, but not burnt for sweet savor. Every meat offering season with salt; the salt of the covenant shall not lack. Offer salt with all thy offerings. Guidance: Leaven puffs up, honey ferments — both image corruption. Salt preserves and stings — image of covenant faithfulness. Eat the honey at home; bring the salt to God.

No meat offering with leaven; neither leaven nor honey burned on the altar. As oblation of firstfruits offer them, but not burnt for sweet savor. Every meat offering season with salt; the salt of the covenant shall not lack. Offer salt with all thy offerings. Guidance: Leaven puffs up, honey ferments — both image corruption. Salt preserves and stings — image of covenant faithfulness. Eat the honey at home; bring the salt to God.

No meat offering with leaven; neither leaven nor honey burned on the altar. As oblation of firstfruits offer them, but not burnt for sweet savor. Every meat offering season with salt; the salt of the covenant shall not lack. Offer salt with all thy offerings. Guidance: Leaven puffs up, honey ferments — both image corruption. Salt preserves and stings — image of covenant faithfulness. Eat the honey at home; bring the salt to God.

If thou offer a meat offering of firstfruits — green ears of corn dried by fire, beaten out of full ears. Put oil, lay frankincense — a meat offering. The priest burns the memorial — beaten corn, oil, frankincense — fire offering to the LORD. Guidance: Even the first green ears can be offered. God doesn't wait for the harvest to be in to receive thanksgiving. The earliest fruit, parched and beaten, is enough — because it is firstfruits.
